title: SC to reconsider order denying bail to Umar Khalid, larger bench to hear matter
content: The Supreme Court on Friday chose not to comment on observations questioning its earlier bail denial to activists Umar Khalid and Sharjeel Imam. While hearing bail pleas for other riot accused, the bench indicated it might grant interim bail, but will consider Delhi Police's plea for a larger bench review on UAPA bail norms.

title: Iran’s rapid military rebuild: 7 weapons Tehran can mass-produce in months
content: Fresh US intelligence suggests Iran has resumed military production, including drones, much sooner than anticipated, raising concerns about its rapid arsenal regeneration. Tehran's strategy of decentralized manufacturing and reliance on commercially available components allows for swift replenishment of weapons like Shahed drones and Fateh missiles, even after recent strikes.

title: Twisha death case: Husband wants to surrender before court, says lawyer; why MP govt objected
content: Accused husband Samarth Singh has informed the Madhya Pradesh high court of his willingness to surrender in the Twisha Sharma dowry death case. His lawyer requested the trial court to hear his bail plea on the surrender day, a move opposed by the Solicitor General. The hearing also addressed a plea for a second post-mortem examination.

title: ‘This is not MS Dhoni’s CSK’- Ashwin’s brutal reality check after disaster campaign
content: Ravichandran Ashwin believes Chennai Super Kings must invest in quality cricketers and a balanced squad for future IPL seasons. He stated the franchise can no longer rely on past strategies, emphasizing the need for experienced bowlers and spinners. Ashwin also questioned if the current CSK squad is suited for Chepauk's batting conditions, suggesting a more patient approach is required.

title: Rajya Sabha polls on June 18: NDA may lose seat, Congress set to gain; TVK debut likely
content: Biennial elections for 26 Rajya Sabha seats will be held on June 18, with the BJP-led NDA expected to lose one seat. The Congress-led opposition could see its tally improve to five seats. The elections will cover 12 states, with bypolls also scheduled for Maharashtra and Tamil Nadu.

title: American billionaire Bill Ackman on why he sold all his Google stocks to buy Microsoft
content: Billionaire investor Bill Ackman has clarified that Pershing Square's sale of Alphabet (Google) shares was not a bearish call on the company. Ackman said his hedge fund offloaded its long-held Google stake to fund a fresh $2.1 billion Microsoft position, betting on the software giant's AI prospects, Azure cloud growth, and Copilot adoption after a steep stock decline this year.

title: 'Marwa denge tujhe': Cockroach Janta Party founder receives death threats
content: Founder of the satirical Cockroach Janta Party, Abhijeet Dipke, has reported receiving death threats on WhatsApp, urging him to shut down the viral social media outfit or join the BJP. His parents have also voiced concerns about his safety and potential arrest due to the party's rapid online popularity.

title: 'Eternal will be memories': Guardiola to leave Man City after decade of dominance
content: Guardiola, who still had one year left on his contract, will take charge of his final match against Aston Villa in the Premier League on Sunday.

title: Trump to send 5,000 additional troops to fortress Poland to further strengthen Nato's eastern flank
content: The US is bolstering its presence in Poland with an additional 5,000 troops, recognizing Warsaw's escalating importance in regional security. Poland, already hosting 10,000 American soldiers, is rapidly militarizing, investing heavily in advanced weaponry from the US and South Korea.

title: Mark Zuckerberg's email to employees has a 'goodbye note' for those laid off
content: Meta has initiated significant layoffs, sending emails to thousands of employees whose positions are being eliminated.  Company leadership confirmed the decision to reduce headcount for increased efficiency.  Meta CEO Mark Zuckerberg also sent a company-wide memo expressing gratitude to departing employees and outlining future company direction.  He assured remaining staff that no further company-wide layoffs are expected this year.

title: Biodiversity begins at home: How local action can create global impact
content: Biodiversity is crucial for human survival, yet it's declining rapidly due to human actions. India is strengthening its conservation efforts, with local communities playing a vital role. Initiatives like restoring coastal ecosystems and creating micro-forests demonstrate how grassroots actions, empowered by traditional knowledge and community institutions, significantly contribute to global biodiversity goals and sustainable livelihoods.

title: ‘Now that cremation is over...’: Ex-cricketer’s shocking disclosure about commentary
content: Former cricketer Laxman Sivaramakrishnan revealed he was compelled to commentate shortly after his brother's cremation, highlighting immense professional pressure. He also cited this and alleged colour discrimination as reasons for his recent retirement from the BCCI commentary panel, detailing past struggles with mental health and appearance-based bias.

title: Excluding family from your Will won't make it invalid, Supreme Court rules
content: The Supreme Court has underscored the legal rights of individuals to dictate their estate plans, stating that a Will is not automatically deemed suspicious simply because it omits heirs like a spouse or children.

title: Google co-founder Sergey Brin donates $500,000 to group fighting ‘overpaid CEO tax’
content: Google co-founder Sergey Brin is injecting significant funds into San Francisco's local politics, backing a business-friendly tax proposal while opposing a controversial "Overpaid CEO Tax."  This move, following his relocation from Silicon Valley, reflects Brin's broader campaign against wealth redistribution measures, which he links to his personal experience fleeing socialism.

title: ‘Bringing war back to Russia’: Ukraine strikes Yaroslavl oil refinery
content: Ukraine has escalated its offensive by targeting a Russian oil refinery situated in Yaroslavl, roughly 700 kilometers away from its own territory, as declared by President Zelenskyy. He positioned this response as a fair counteraction in the ongoing conflict, propelling the conflict back into Russian territory.

title: TANCET 2026 result released, scorecards to be released on this date
content: Anna University has announced the TANCET Result 2026. Candidates can check their scores on the official website. Over 30,000 students appeared for the TANCET and CEETA PG examinations. Scorecards will be available from May 27. Profile corrections are permitted before downloading the scorecard. Further updates on counselling will be provided.

title: Twisha Sharma death: Mother-in-law’s lawyer loses cool, beats vehicle bonnet; watch
content: Lawyer for Twisha Sharma's mother-in-law, Giribala Singh, Advocate Enosh George, had a heated exchange with journalists outside the residence. After his vehicle allegedly ran over his foot, George accused reporters of creating a dangerous situation and questioned the impact of their legal responses in the ongoing dowry death investigation.

title: Rajya Sabha polls 2026: Election commission announces date for Upper House elections
content: Polling for the Rajya Sabha elections will take  between 9 am and 4 pm, with counting scheduled to begin at 5 pm the same day. The Election Commission said the entire poll process will conclude by June 20. The Commission clarified that voters will be allowed to use only the integrated violet sketch pens supplied by the Returning Officer for marking preferences on ballot papers. Use of any other pen during voting will not be permitted.

title: Delhi dowry death case: Nail scratches, ruptured eardrum, final SOS call — years of torture
content: Veena Kumari died after falling from her terrace in Delhi. Her family alleges dowry harassment and abuse led to her death. They claim repeated demands for more dowry resulted in beatings and humiliation. The family sought help from village elders. Police have arrested her husband and his brother.

title: Hormuz crisis: Venezuela becomes India’s third-largest crude oil supplier in May
content: Venezuela has surged to become India's third-largest crude oil supplier this May, surpassing Saudi Arabia and the US. Indian refiners are boosting purchases of cheaper Venezuelan heavy crude due to its attractive economics and compatibility with complex refineries. This shift comes as West Asia conflict disrupts traditional shipping routes, prompting India to diversify its oil sourcing.

title: Dry wells, dry fields: Water stress peaks in Maharashtra’s rain-shadow villages
content: Elderly residents in Maharashtra's drought-prone Man Desh region are undertaking arduous journeys for water as younger generations migrate due to agrarian distress and dwindling livelihoods. The crisis is exacerbated by underreported shortages and falling reservoir levels, with authorities deploying water tankers to mitigate the severe scarcity across several districts.

title: How industrial belts, dense colonies and unplanned settlements turned into Delhi’s hottest zones
content: A new report reveals over 75% of Delhi's land area experienced recurring heat stress between 2015 and 2024. Industrial belts, dense residential areas, and unplanned settlements are identified as the hottest zones, with land surface temperatures reaching up to 60.7 degrees Celsius. The study highlights weakening natural cooling systems and recommends recognizing heat as a disaster.

title: Copying Vaibhav Sooryavanshi would be 'foolish', says Devdutt Padikkal
content: Devdutt Padikkal lauded teenage sensation Vaibhav Sooryavanshi as a "rare talent," deeming any attempt to replicate his unique batting style "foolish." Sooryavanshi's explosive 93 off 38 balls made him the youngest to score 500+ T20 runs in a season, surpassing Padikkal's previous record. Both left-handers have been impactful players for their franchises.

title: Delhi HC pulls up WFI for declaring Vinesh Phogat ‘ineligible’ for domestic events
content: The Delhi high court has strongly criticized the Wrestling Federation of India for declaring Vinesh Phogat ineligible for domestic events. The court urged the Centre to form an expert committee to assess Phogat's status and ensure her participation in Asian Games trials, emphasizing the protection of athletes and the sport's larger interest.

title: Jhalmuri vendor who served PM Modi facing death threats from Pak, B'desh
content: A jhalmuri vendor from Jhargram, who served Prime Minister Modi, claims to be receiving threatening calls from Pakistan and Bangladesh.  The vendor, Vikram Sao, has informed the police, who have provided security.  Sao, the sole breadwinner, fears for his safety while cycling and has appealed to the PM for protection after his interaction went viral.

title: Was Cole Palmer snubbed from World Cup squad because Tuchel doesn't like his personality?
content: Thomas Tuchel has reportedly left Cole Palmer out of England’s World Cup squad after concerns over the forward’s personality and suitability for tournament football. The Chelsea star was one of the biggest names missing from Tuchel’s latest squad announcement despite scoring ten goals during the season. The England manager is believed to be focusing strongly on team chemistry and leadership while building his squad for the World Cup.

title: ‘Pushed off terrace for dowry’: Deepika Nagar’s family demands fast-track justice
content: A 24-year-old woman died after falling from her in-laws' terrace in Greater Noida, with her family alleging dowry torture and demanding a fast-track trial. Police have arrested four individuals, including her husband and in-laws, while three others remain absconding. Autopsy revealed severe injuries, and viscera samples are undergoing forensic analysis.

title: 'All negativity will go away, if...': Ponting responds to social media backlash
content: Punjab Kings coach Ricky Ponting dismisses social media criticism amid a six-match winless streak, asserting the team remains focused. He believes qualifying for the playoffs will silence negativity and urges players to recall their early-season fearless brand of cricket to revive their fading hopes against Lucknow Super Giants.

title: Lucknow doctor accused of raping ailing student after sending father, staff out of OT
content: A doctor at a private Lucknow hospital is accused of raping a female student after administering a sedative injection during treatment. The student alleged the doctor called her into the operation theatre alone and assaulted her after giving her an intoxicating injection. Police have registered a case and detained the doctor for questioning.

title: 'People offering condolences': Twisha's mother-in-law denies speaking to influencial people
content: Retired judge Giribala Singh denies allegations of contacting influential figures after her daughter-in-law Twisha Sharma's death. She claims calls were for condolences and accuses local police of pressure. Sharma's family alleges dowry harassment and abetment to suicide. The husband, Samarth Singh, remains absconding. The case involves FIRs under the Bharatiya Nyay Sanhita and Dowry Prohibition Act.

title: Sudharsan, Gill script IPL history, break multiple records in CSK demolition
content: Sai Sudharsan and Shubman Gill etched their names in IPL history with remarkable performances against Chennai Super Kings. Both batters surpassed 600 runs for the second consecutive season, a feat achieved by only a select few. Their dominant opening partnership also saw them climb various T20 and IPL record lists.

title: 'Muttatthengu' in TN: DMK takes 'bent coconut tree' jibe at VCK over CM Vijay's cabinet
content: A literary spat erupts in Tamil Nadu politics as DMK leader A Raja's 'coconut tree' metaphor targets VCK and IUML joining CM Vijay's new government. VCK and the ruling TVK hit back, accusing the DMK of arrogance and questioning their own past alliances and commitment to social justice.

title: Diabetes in the scrolling era: Are screens becoming a hidden risk?
content: Prolonged screen time poses significant risks for individuals with diabetes, contributing to sedentary lifestyles, poor sleep, and increased snacking. This can lead to uncontrolled blood sugar, weight gain, and long-term complications like diabetic retinopathy. Experts advise incorporating movement breaks, limiting screen time before bed, and mindful technology use to manage these risks.

title: RBI steps in with heavy dollar sales to defend rupee: Report
content: The Reserve Bank of India aggressively intervened, selling billions to defend the rupee against sustained pressure.  This significant dollar selling, even before markets opened, helped the currency strengthen.  Elevated oil prices remain a key concern, with policymakers considering further measures, including a potential interest rate hike, to stabilize the rupee.

title: 'Content, not individuals': SC tones down remarks against academicians in NCERT row
content: The Supreme Court has assured three academics that harsh remarks against them in a previous order will be deleted. These remarks stemmed from a Class 8 NCERT chapter on judicial corruption. The court clarified its concern was the chapter's content, not the individuals, emphasizing the need for balanced academic discussions on the judiciary while respecting constitutional frameworks.

title: 'Sometimes people abuse so much': Ashwin defends Gaikwad amid criticism
content: Ravichandran Ashwin has defended Ruturaj Gaikwad against fan criticism following Chennai Super Kings' IPL 2026 playoff exit. Ashwin urged supporters to stop abusing the young captain, emphasizing that poor form is common and highlighting the increased competitiveness of the IPL. He noted Gaikwad's struggles as both batter and captain during the season.

title: ‘No sacrifice of cows, calves’: Delhi govt warns against illegal slaughter before Bakrid
content: Delhi Minister Kapil Mishra has directed strict enforcement of animal welfare laws for Bakrid.  He emphasized zero tolerance for illegal animal transport, slaughter, and cruelty.  Sacrifices are permitted only at designated sites, with a complete ban on cows and camels.  Public spaces are off-limits for animal trade and sacrifice, with swift action promised against violators.

title: ‘Ghar pe toh judge Twisha hi hogi’: Playful wedding videos surface as police hunt absconding husband
content: A wedding trailer of Twisha Sharma and Samarth Singh, once a joyous glimpse into their marriage, now haunts online amid a sensational dowry death case. Twisha's family accuses her husband and retired judge mother-in-law of harassment. The video contrasts sharply with the grim reality of police investigations and court battles, as the husband remains absconding.

title: ‘Only MS will know, but...’: CSK coach drops big hint on Dhoni’s IPL future
content: MS Dhoni's IPL future rests solely with him, according to CSK bowling coach Eric Simons. Despite a leg injury sidelining him from IPL 2026, Dhoni is reportedly striking the ball brilliantly in nets. Simons emphasised that Dhoni will only return if he feels he can contribute fully, prioritising the team's best interests over personal milestones.

title: Head to head: India and Pakistan's nuclear missile arsenal
content: In a dramatic display of military strategy, Operation Sindoor witnessed India targeting Pakistani airbases and intercepting their missiles, ultimately facilitating a quick resolution. This incident emphasizes the delicate nuclear dynamics between India and Pakistan, two nations holding nearly equivalent stockpiles. Despite their varied approaches to weapon usage, both countries utilize a nuclear triad, essential for their deterrence capabilities.

title: 'You have to save yourself': Sanjay Dutt’s daughter Trishala on her painful childhood
content: Trishala Dutt, daughter of Sanjay Dutt, reveals a difficult childhood in New York. She faced racism and bullying from a young age. The loss of her mother at eight deeply affected her. Dutt found solace in journaling and later, a pivotal conversation with her aunt empowered her. She pursued psychology and became a therapist, using her experiences to help others.

title: Umar Khalid gets 3-day interim bail for mother’s surgery
content: Umar Khalid has been granted three days of interim bail by the Delhi High Court to be with his mother for her surgery. He will be released from June 1 to June 3, with strict conditions including staying within Delhi-NCR and only visiting the hospital. The court's decision came after a trial court rejected his temporary relief plea.

title: RO systems should be maintained properly to be effective, says study
content: The survey covered 262 samples from 216 households in Chennai, looking at both treated and source water to assess the effectiveness of household water treatment systems. The findings show that 31% of post-RO samples still contained E. coli

title: Will TVK government take ‘firm stand’ on Mekedatu project, asks Palaniswami
content: The AIADMK leader, in a post on social media, said he raised the question because the TVK regime is being run with the support of the Congress, which is the ruling party in Karnataka

title: UDF, LDF in Kalamassery municipality spar over gaps in waste management at Ernakulam Govt. Medical College
content: A resolution, passed by the UDF, blamed the hospital management for not taking necessary measures in ensuring proper waste management as per the directives issued by the Kerala State Pollution Control Board

title: Kasaragod district administration prepares railway master plan to end travel hardships
content: District Collector Arjun Pandian has formally written to railway authorities seeking the extension of major train services terminating at Kannur and Mangaluru to Kasaragod, restoration of discontinued passenger services, and stoppages for key superfast trains at Kasaragod railway station

title: Political activity set to pick up in Karnataka with notification of elections to four Rajya Sabha seats
content: The elections have been necessitated by completion of the term of former Prime Minister H.D. Deve Gowda and Leader of Opposition in Rajya Sabha Mallikarjuna Kharge

title: Vizianagaram goldsmiths seek Centre’s clarity on gold buying amid week-long protests
content: Goldsmiths in Rajam, Cheepurupalli and Vizianagaram have been agitating for a week, saying Prime Minister Narendra Modi’s call not to buy gold for a year has hit their trade

title: Congress alleges attempted cover-up of NEET paper leak, demands accountability from PM and Education Minister
content: The Congress also said that the National Testing Agency (NTA) has become a ‘National Trauma Agency’ and accused the government of colluding with the paper leak mafia to suppress the truth of irregularities and fraud

title: Muslim side moves Supreme Court challenging Madhya Pradesh HC order in Bhojshala complex dispute case
content: The appeal filed by the mosque’s caretaker, Qazi Moinuddin, has challenged the May 15 order of the High Court

title: Maternity need not be proved through DNA test before donating kidney to child: Madras High Court
content: Justice G.R. Swaminathan rules in favour of comes to the rescue a West Bengal-based mother and son, who intended to undergo the transplantation surgery in Chennai

title: NEET-UG paper leak row: Congress leader Ashok Gehlot slams NTA’s ‘irresponsible’ stand
content: NTA Chairman Pradeep Joshi had told a Parliamentary Committee that he did not believe the NEET paper had been leaked, the former Rajasthan CM said

title: Infants in Telangana have better survival odds, IMR at 17 against national average 24
content: Kerala remained the best-performing large State in the country with an IMR of 8, followed by Delhi and Tamil Nadu at 11 each

title: Madhya Pradesh High Court permits second postmortem of Twisha Sharma, says family
content: While her postmortem was done at AIIMS Bhopal, her family had sought a second autopsy, citing discrepancies.

title: Tripura government cancels summer vacation in colleges
content: “In the interest of the students, summer vacation for 26 days for all colleges, scheduled from May 26, is hereby withdrawn,” Higher Education Director Animesh Debbarma said

title: ‘Are cartoons in NCERT textbooks proper?’ Supreme Court asks retired judge panel to review
content: The order came after Solicitor General Tushar Mehta raised the issue and said 'textbook is not a place where you use cartoons'

title: Erstwhile BBMP’s stray dog feeding programme in Bengaluru on backburner
content: The project has been put on the backburner mainly due to a lack of interest, which activists say ‘should not have happened’

title: 22 Ministers aged below 50: Vijay-led Cabinet signals generational shift in Tamil Nadu politics
content: Data on TVK Ministers, as on May 21, show that the Cabinet of 51-year-old Chief Minister C. Joseph Vijay has the youngest average age among the three governments examined, while the 2021 Cabinet of M.K. Stalin had the oldest

title: Centre extends NCB chief Anurag Garg's tenure till July 2027
content: The Appointments Committee of the Cabinet has approved extension in Anurag Garg's deputation tenure as Director General of Narcotics Control Bureau

title: Arunachal Pradesh CM Pema Khandu calls for collective action to protect biodiversity
content: CM Pema Khandu’s message assumes significance for Arunachal Pradesh, one of India's most ecologically rich States, and part of the Eastern Himalayan biodiversity hotspot

title: Govt. moves Pregabalin to Schedule H1 following reports of drug abuse
content: Prescribed for the treatment of chronic pain, neuropathies, fibromyalgia, and certain neurological conditions, Pregabalin has been misused for its sedative, euphoric, and dissociative effects.

title: Govt. to ensure no ‘unnatural demographic changes’ in the country, each infiltrator to be expelled: Amit Shah
content: Addressing the Border Security Force (BSF) Investiture Ceremony and the Rustamji Memorial Lecture, Union Home Minister said a ‘high-powered demography mission will be announced soon and it will make vulnerable locations available to BSF’

title: Twisha Sharma death: Madhya Pradesh government proposes to transfer case to CBI
content: The family of Twisha Sharma, the 33-year-old model turned actor accused her in-laws of pushing their daughter to death

title: Historic shift: Vijay’s Cabinet marks Tamil Nadu’s best Scheduled Caste representation till date
content: This is the first time that eight people from the SC community have been included simultaneously in the Tamil Nadu Cabinet

title: Ajmer Sharif cleric demands national animal status for cow, ban on slaughter, beef export
content: The cleric's appeal days ahead of Eid-ul-Azha on May 28. Mr. Chishti said that the cow holds deep religious significance for the Hindu community and deserves respect and constitutional protection

title: IIM Shillong joins elite league of global business schools
content: The institute received the Association to Advance Collegiate Schools of Business accreditation recently

title: Latin Catholic Church urges Kerala govt to fast-track relief for fishers ahead of ‘rough season’
content: After a closed-door meeting between Fisheries Minister Abdul Gafoor and Thiruvananthapuram Archbishop Thomas J Netto, Church spokesperson Fr Beed Manoj Amado said “rough season” was imminent and the government should fast-track relief measures to alleviate the fishing community’s hardship

title: Karnataka State Wakf Council urges government to ensure protection during Bakrid celebrations
content: The council urged the State Government and Home Department to take stringent action against those engaging in threats, violence, vehicle check, assault, or attempts to disturb communal harmony

title: West Bengal government notifies free bus travel for women in State-run buses from June 1
content: The Transport Department notification says that a smart card with QR code, photograph, and beneficiary details will be issued to all eligible women upon application

title: As water level rises, Kerala’s Karapuzha dam shutters to be opened as precautionary measure
content: District Collector D.R. Meghasree, who is also the chairperson of District Disaster Management Authority, has granted permission to raise the shutters on Saturday as part of monsoon preparedness measures

title: Rajasthan High Court orders panchayat, local body polls by July 31
content: A Division Bench headed by Acting Chief Justice Sanjeev Prakash Sharma also directed the OBC Commission to submit its report by June 20.

title: CM Vijay-led Cabinet in Tamil Nadu expands to 33; Congress joins government after 59 years
content: Mr. Vijay’s Cabinet is likely to undergo one more round of expansion, as the TVK has extended an open invitation to the VCK and IUML, both of which contested as part of the DMK-led Secular Progressive Alliance (SPA) and have two MLAs each

title: Former Speaker Dhanapal announces exit from AIADMK
content: After attending the swearing-in of his son and Rasipuram legislator, D. Logesh Tamilsevan, as Minister in the Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam (TVK)-led Cabinet, at the Lok Bhavan in Chennai, Mr. Dhanapal told reporters he had not been given “importance” by the AIADMK leadership

title: Hyderabad Ponzi case: Enforcement Directorate arrests Nowhera Shaik
content: The officials said she was living in Gurugram under a ‘fake identity using forged documents’ and was produced before Prevention of Money Laundering Act court in Hyderabad

title: VCK, IUML join Vijay’s Cabinet in Tamil Nadu
content: With VCK’s Vanni Arasu and IUML’s Shahjahan on board, Vijay’s Cabinet in Tamil Nadu now has 35 Ministers

title: Stray dog euthanasia: Bengaluru civic bodies to have separate committees to assess requirement and ensure transparency
content: The Supreme Court underscored that the authorities concerned must undertake euthanasia only after due assessment by qualified veterinary experts and strictly in accordance with applicable statutory protocols

title: Indonesia tightens control over key commodities in major trade takeover, influencing global exports
content: The new regulation announced by Indonesian President Prabowo Subianto mandates that a recently set up state-owned enterprise will handle the country's exports

title: Centre asks States to step up Ebola preparedness after WHO-declares global health emergency
content: The Ministry has also circulated a Standard Operating Procedure (SOP) covering disease surveillance, sample collection, storage and referral mechanisms for suspected Ebola cases

title: Kerala Cabinet: Latin Catholic Church remains dissatisfied with exclusion; Fisheries Minister meets Archbishop
content: IUML leader and Fisheries Minister Abdul Gafoor’s mission to iron out difficulties with Church leadership in a closed-door meet with Thiruvananthapuram Archbishop Thomas J. Netto appears to have yielded mixed results

title: UCC, anti-radicalisation measures among Assam government’s priorities
content: Governor Lakshman Prasad Acharya outlines BJP-led government’s five-year vision, including border fencing, anti-infiltration measures and protection of indigenous communities

title: PM Modi, Cyprus President Nikos Christodoulides hold bilateral meeting
content: External Affairs Minister S. Jaishankar, National Security Advisor Ajit Doval, and Foreign Secretary Vikram Misri are among those present

title: Ebola surveillance stepped up at Kochi airport after emergency meet
content: A Cochin International Airport Limited spokesperson said a 24-hour monitoring system has now been activated at the airport as part of efforts to detect possible symptoms early and strengthen coordinated public health response measures

title: Supreme Court grants bail to J&K man booked under UAPA after five years in custody as undertrial
content: The bail order comes a few days after Supreme Court observed in another case that the slogan ‘bail is the rule’ is not an empty one

title: After CM Vijay’s order to revive Amma Canteens, AIADMK hopes for comeback of other legacy schemes
content: AIADMK functionaries have urged the revival of other flagship welfare schemes introduced during the Dravidian party’s previous tenures

title: NCERT book row: Supreme Court modifies earlier order concerning 3 academics
content: The Court also observed that its comments were made in context of the contents and not the individuals

title: PM's gifts: Muga silk stole for Italy PM, Ladakhi stole for Sweden PM, Kesar mangoes for UAE President
content: PM Modi gifts traditional Indian artifacts and delicacies to global leaders during his five-nation diplomatic tour

title: DMK faces a challenge as allies set to join TVK government
content: While the Congress abandoned the DMK soon after polls and joined Chief Minister C Joseph Vijay’s Cabinet on Thursday, the IUML and VCK are set to join the Ministry on Friday, signalling the end of their relations with the DMK

title: CM Yogi asks officials to take investment to every U.P. district
content: CM Yogi Adityanath emphasised expanding the land bank, adding that the incentives provided for investment should be turned into events to honour investors and boost their morale.

title: 2025 landslide in Shimla Act of God, NHAI tells NGT, rejects compensation claim
content: NHAI submitted its reply against plea filed in January, claiming that heavy construction work being undertaken by the authority and its concessionaire, without properly strengthening a slope, had damaged agricultural land in Shimla rural tehsil, causing landslides

title: Room for reconciliation within AIADMK
content: A senior leader of the rebels, who include former Ministers C.Ve. Shanmugam, S.P. Velumani and G. Hari, says that efforts were on “from the beginning” to bring the two camps together

title: Sharmila criticises Modi over Melody toffees gifted to Italian PM Giorgia Meloni
content: The APCC president calls the gesture “an insane and insensitive display of showmanship” and says BJP leaders hand out chocolates while people struggle with rising prices

title: Shashi Tharoor slams X ban on ‘Cockroach Janata Party’
content: Congress MP Shashi Tharoor criticised the withholding of CJP’s X account, saying democracies need space for satire, dissent and youth frustration

title: Why your daily cup of tea is getting costlier in Kerala
content: Rising commercial cooking gas prices have raised tea shop costs. Come June 1, Milma will also hike milk prices by ₹4/litre, further compounding the problem

title: Bhagwant Mann Faces Backlash Over 'Misinterpreted' Top Court Dog Ruling
content: Punjab Governor Gulab Chand Kataria also urged caution, stating that any such exercise must strictly follow Supreme Court guidelines.

title: Twisha Sharma Death Updates: Samarth Singh Withdraws Anticipatory Bail Plea, Likely To Surrender
content: Twisha Sharma Death Updates: The case came under scrutiny as the Madhya Pradesh Police faced criticism for an inefficient probe into the case, while Giribala Singh was accused of exerting undue influence.

title: Opinion: Opinion | India's Oil Reserves: How Long Can They Last As Iran War Continues?
content: From a future energy security perspective, India will have to significantly enhance its SPR capacities from 17 days to a minimum of 45 days on land, with the possibility of another 10-15 days of SPR on sea on tankers.

title: 'Dark Nightmare': Commuters After 50-Minute Rush-Hour Glitch On Mumbai Metro
content: Passengers reported experiencing immense mental stress and inconvenience during this period and were unable to even establish contact with Metro staff

title: Video: Giribala Singh's Lawyer Bangs On Car, Shouts As Reporters Surround Him
content: Enosh George's anger increased manifold as his foot came under the tyre of the car in which Giribala Singh was sitting.

title: 'Say Jai Shri Ram In Attendance Calls': Karnataka BJP Leaders Amid Hijab Debate
content: The hijab controversy first erupted in Karnataka in 2022 after Muslim students in Udupi protested restrictions on wearing hijabs inside classrooms

title: NPS Swasthya Explained: Can It Replace Your Health Insurance?
content: Experts say NPS Swasthya is not designed to replace health insurance. Instead, it may work best as a second layer of financial protection.

title: Is UAE Building Anti-Drone Shield Around Oil Facilities Amid Iran Attacks?
content: Iran has increasingly singled out the Emirates in its war messaging as it attacked the United States and Israeli targets in the Middle East, repeatedly targeting locations across the country, including oil facilities.

title: "Getting Threats From Pak, Bangladesh": Man Who Sold Jhalmuri To PM In Bengal
content: PM Modi stopped at Sao's shop for a brief Jhalmuri snack on April 19 during the high-voltage campaign.

title: Winds Of Change In J&K: Rural Girls Train To Join Police, Serve Nation
content: The curriculum is comprehensive and free of cost. Physical endurance, long runs, obstacle drills and personality development.

title: Opposition MLAs Walk Out Over Governor's Address in Assam Assembly
content: The Opposition alleged that several important public issues, including rising prices, were not adequately addressed in the speech

title: Putin May "Regret" Ukraine War: Xi Jinping Told Trump During Beijing Trip
content: Xi Jinping’s alleged comments are significant because China has publicly maintained close ties with Russia since the war began and has avoided openly criticising Vladimir Putin.

title: Opinion: Opinion: TVK Dismantles Dravidian Stereotypes Without Disturbing Tamil Political Positions
content: A young Brahmin man, with or without any political background, winning a seat in Tamil Nadu and subsequently becoming a minister, does not fit the established template of Dravidian politics.

title: Why Does Every Human Voice Sound Different? Scientists Explain
